---
title: "Biomechanics: Soft Tissue, Cardiovascular, and Respiratory Systems"
subtitle: "Track F — Physical Systems"
date: "2025-01-01"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
number-sections: true
---

# Biomechanics: Soft Tissue, Cardiovascular, and Respiratory Systems

**Track**: Track F — Physical Systems

Application of continuum mechanics and fluid dynamics to biological systems with emphasis on soft tissue mechanics, cardiovascular hemodynamics, and respiratory mechanics.

## Table of Contents

1. Mechanics of Biological Soft Tissue
2. Arterial Wall Mechanics
3. Cardiovascular Hemodynamics
4. Respiratory Mechanics
5. Musculoskeletal Biomechanics
6. Brain and Neural Tissue Mechanics
7. Cell Mechanics and Mechanobiology
8. Computational Biomechanics: Patient-Specific Modeling

---

## Chapter 1: Mechanics of Biological Soft Tissue

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 2: Arterial Wall Mechanics

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 3: Cardiovascular Hemodynamics

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 4: Respiratory Mechanics

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 5: Musculoskeletal Biomechanics

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 6: Brain and Neural Tissue Mechanics

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 7: Cell Mechanics and Mechanobiology

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 8: Computational Biomechanics: Patient-Specific Modeling

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*
